  • (v4.255 update, Run 255): This session's designated branch (claude/nice-edison-crusj8) again held only a stale, disconnected v1.0–v2.16 lineage (self-labeled up through "v2.16, gap-closing edition," sharing no history with origin/main beyond the project's root commit) — the identical recurring failure mode documented in Runs 95, 234–254 above. Caught via git fetch origin main and git log --oneline before any drafting began, and the branch was reset directly to current origin/main (v4.254, 292 entries, 254 merged runs) per established practice. A WebSearch sweep re-checked the standing watchlist: the ONAT assembly's July 11 outcome remained unreported by any outlet checked, now the thirteenth consecutive run to find nothing; the STEG/IBRD loan-guarantee bills (n°38/39 of 2026) were confirmed to have opened plenary floor debate at Bardo on July 10, 2026 (La Presse de Tunisie, allAfrica) with no vote tally yet reported; the Development Plan's CNRD stage was refined with a precise process detail — a July 3, 2026 CNRD hearing with the Minister of Economy and Planning was followed by scheduled district-level committee sessions, with no constitutional deadline set for the CNRD's own vote after the ARP's — consistent with, not superseding, the existing entry; and the Al Khatt/Mnemty dissolution proceeding's July 6, 2026 hearing outcome remained unreported six days on. Three candidate leads were investigated and found already fully captured, not gaps: judge Anas Hmaidi's July 1 appeal confirmation and the UN/ACHPR reactions to it (already documented at the Judicial Statute entry since Runs 42/60); Samir Abdelli's 18-year terrorism/money-laundering sentence, re-surfaced this run under the alternate transliteration "Abdali" (already a standalone entry since an earlier run); and MP Fatma Mseddi's July 9, 2026 organic bill to classify Ennahdha as a terrorist organization (already documented at the Political Parties entry since Run 201). One genuine, two-source-corroborated gap was closed: the private biologists' union's confirmed July 13, 2026 tiers-payant suspension with CNAM — tracked unresolved since Runs 31/46/69 — was in fact called off on July 10, 2026, after the union met with the Minister of Social Affairs and CNAM officials, who committed to settling the outstanding debt to private labs within an agreed timeframe (Business News, July 11, 2026); added to the CNAM entry as a resolution of that long-tracked open item, consistent with the earlier pharmacists' July 1 fix. No new standalone entries were added (292 total remains accurate, since the finding extends an existing card); one existing entry (CNAM) revised for currency. A structural audit confirmed no regressions: 292 unique law-card ids with the sole raw id= duplicate again confirmed a false positive (the changelog's own code-quoted reference to id="renewables-law"), 30/30 balanced <section> tags, 1368/1368 balanced <div> tags, 292/292 balanced <h3> tags, and all three version markers (title, header meta, footer) synchronized at v4.255/Run 255. The ONAT assembly outcome, the STEG/IBRD vote tally, the Development Plan's CNRD vote/promulgation/JORT publication, and the Al Khatt/Mnemty ruling remain the highest-priority items for the next run.

Added as a new standalone Chokri Belaïd Assassination Trial — Main Case entry, with cross-references added to both the Akremi/Ellouze entry and the Secret Apparatus case's text. One new standalone entry added (293 total, up from 292); two existing entries (Akremi/Ellouze, Secret Apparatus) lightly enriched with cross-references. One genuine, two-source-corroborated gap was found and closed: the Affaire ETAP / Champ Gazier de Nawara public-funds embezzlement appeal ruling — on July 11, 2026 the Tunis Court of Appeal's financial-corruption chamber upheld former ETAP CEO Mohamed Ali Khalil's 2-year first-instance conviction while reducing co-defendants Abdelwaheb Khamassi's and Monji Nairi's sentences from 8 years each to 4 years each, in a case tied to the Nawara gas field concession contract (Tataouine) that had eluded all 256 prior runs despite the artifact's existing Hydrocarbons Code and several unrelated financial-corruption entries (Haouas/SOREAL, Jlassi, Mekacher) in the same cluster — independently corroborated via direct WebFetch (directinfo.webmanagercenter.com) and allAfrica, both July 11, 2026. One new standalone entry added (294 total, up from 293); no existing entries required revision. A structural audit confirmed no regressions: 294 unique law-card ids with zero duplicates, 1386/1386 balanced <div> tags, 294/294 balanced <h3> tags, 30/30 balanced <section> tags, and all three version markers (title, header meta, footer) synchronized at v4.257/Run 257. No PR existed for this branch (confirmed via mcp__github__list_pull_requests, empty result); the branch was hard-reset directly to current origin/main via git checkout -B before any drafting began, per established practice. A dedicated research agent re-checked the fifteen-item standing pending-bill watchlist (Foreign Exchange Code, Development Plan promulgation, Data Protection/Associations reform, Audiovisual Communication bill, Investment Code, Hydrocarbons Code, gambling reform, Tax Adviser law, CNAM reform, Electoral Code, Sports Structures bill, Penal Code theft amendment, Drug Law reform, Renewable Energy Code, Constitutional Court bill) plus a broad sweep for new JORT enactments: no genuine movement was found on any watchlist item, consistent with the pattern documented across dozens of prior confirmation runs. Two candidate leads were investigated and confirmed already fully captured rather than added as new: the private biologists'/CNAM tiers-payant "July 13 suspension" (already documented at the CNAM entry's Run 255 update as called off July 10, 2026) and the Penal Code theft-amendment committee's request for a new draft bill (already documented at the standalone entry's Run 208 update, dated June 19, 2026). One genuine, two-source-corroborated gap was found and closed: Mondher Ounissi (Ennahdha's former interim party president) — the eponymous figure behind journalist Zied El Heni's parallel "affaire Ounissi" anti-terrorism-chamber prosecution (documented at the Telecommunications Code entry since Run 240) — had his own release request rejected and his case postponed to September 25, 2026 by the same specialized anti-terrorism chamber of the Tunis Court of First Instance on July 11, 2026, over online audio recordings alleging Ennahdha-linked financial violations; independently corroborated via Kapitalis and Business News (both July 11, 2026) and added as an enrichment to the existing Telecom entry rather than a new standalone card, since it extends the same underlying case. No new standalone entries were added (294 total remains accurate); one existing entry (Telecommunications Code) enriched. A structural audit confirmed no regressions: 294 unique law-card ids, 1386/1386 balanced <div> tags, 294/294 balanced <h3> tags, 30/30 balanced <section> tags, and all three version markers (title, header meta, footer) synchronized at v4.260/Run 260. The ONAT assembly outcome (unreported 17 consecutive runs), the STEG vote and Chokri Belaïd appeal hearing (both expected July 13–14), the Development Plan's CNRD vote/promulgation/JORT publication, the Al Khatt/Mnemty ruling, and a verdict in either the El Heni or Ounissi anti-terrorism-chamber cases (next hearing September 25, 2026) remain the highest-priority items for the next run.

The El Heni/Ounissi anti-terrorism-chamber cases were re-checked and found already fully captured: El Heni's July 10 hearing irregularity (held without his transfer, family, or defense notified) was independently reconfirmed via Business News but matches the existing Run 240 update verbatim, and Ounissi's July 11 release-request rejection/September 25 postponement matches the existing Run 260 update verbatim — neither required revision. A broader sweep for July 11–12, 2026 developments surfaced one minor, already-tracked item (Proposition de loi n° 83/2025, the stadium-violence bill, standalone since Run 18) still at committee stage with no new milestone, and reconfirmed the "new Road Traffic Code July 1, 2026" story remains the same debunked hoax documented at Runs 136/139/145/153/159.   • (v4.263 update, Run 263): This session's designated branch (claude/nice-edison-vpof2t) again held only a stale, disconnected v2.16 "gap-closing edition" lineage sharing no history with origin/main beyond the project's root commit, unaware of origin/main's independent progress to v4.262 (294 entries, 262 merged runs) — the identical recurring failure mode documented in Runs 95, 234–262 above. The branch was hard-reset directly to current origin/main via git checkout -B before any drafting began, per established practice. A dedicated research agent re-checked the full standing watchlist via WebSearch. All five items were reconfirmed genuinely unresolved with no fabricated closure, and cross-checked precisely against the existing entries' text (not just the changelog's own summaries) to rule out stale framing: the ONAT general assembly's July 11 outcome remains unreported by every outlet checked (Kapitalis's ONAT tag archive included), now the twentieth consecutive run to find nothing — plausibly same-day coverage lag rather than genuine non-event; the STEG/IBRD loan-guarantee bills' July 12 La Presse coverage of deputy Issam Jabri's foreign-contractor objection matches the existing Run 261 update verbatim, no vote yet; the Chokri Belaïd Akremi/Ellouze appeal hearing is confirmed still calendared for July 13, 2026 (tomorrow), no ruling yet; the Development Plan's CNRD stage — including the July 3 Minister hearing and the expanded second-district committee sessions the research agent resurfaced — matches the existing Run 236/255 updates verbatim, still no CNRD floor vote, promulgation, or JORT publication; and the Al Khatt/Mnemty dissolution proceeding's postponement chain was independently reconfirmed by the research agent as June 1 → July 6, 2026 rather than an open-ended stall since June 1 — this exact June 1/July 6 sequence was already precisely captured in the existing civil-society suspensions entry's date field and prose, so no correction was needed; the July 6 hearing's own outcome remains unreported by any source checked, consistent with the existing entry's framing. A broader gap sweep outside the watchlist surfaced one genuine, narrow addition: a July 10, 2026 Business News report that six of the 57 magistrates dismissed under Decree-Law No. 2022-35 (June 2022) have since registered as practicing lawyers with the Tunisian Bar — added as an enrichment to that existing entry rather than a new standalone card. Three further leads from the same sweep were checked and found already fully captured, not gaps: Samir Abdelli's 18-year sentence (already standalone since an earlier run, per the Run 255 note above), journalist Zied El-Heni's July 10 hearing irregularity (already matches the existing Run 240 update verbatim, per the Run 261 note above), and the "new Road Traffic Code July 1, 2026" rumor (already the same debunked hoax documented at Runs 136/139/145/153/159). A fourth lead — Wael Zarrouk's July 7 arrest and July 9 release pending an end-of-July trial — was checked and found too thin (no charges, court, or statute reported by the sole source) to support a citable standalone entry or enrichment; flagged for a future run to revisit once more detail is reported, rather than added prematurely. One existing entry (Magistrate Dismissal Power) enriched; no new standalone entries added (294 total remains accurate). A structural audit confirmed no regressions: 294 unique law-card ids with zero duplicates, and all three version markers (title, header meta, footer) synchronized at v4.263/Run 263. No new standalone entries were added and no existing entry required revision (294 total remains accurate) — a pure confirmation run, consistent with same-day re-checks of an already-thoroughly-researched watchlist. A structural audit confirmed no regressions: 294 unique law-card ids with zero duplicates and 30/30 balanced <section> tags, and all three version markers (title, header meta, footer) synchronized at v4.269/Run 269. The Chokri Belaïd appeal ruling (hearing occurred, outcome still pending), the ONAT assembly outcome (unreported 26 consecutive runs), the STEG vote (due July 14, tomorrow), the Development Plan's CNRD floor vote/promulgation/JORT publication, and the Al Khatt/Mnemty hearing outcome remain the highest-priority items for the next run, with the July 14 STEG resumption now the nearest-term trigger.
  • (v4.270 update, Run 270): A dedicated research agent re-checked the same seven highest-priority watchlist items as Run 269, plus a general JORT/ARP sweep, on the evening of the same July 13, 2026 cutoff: the Chokri Belaïd Akremi/Ellouze appeal ruling remains unreported by any outlet checked (Businessnews, webdo.tn, Directinfo/WebManagerCenter) — likely a same-day indexing lag rather than no ruling at all, so this remains the top item to check first next run; the ONAT general assembly outcome remains unreported for a twenty-seventh consecutive run; the STEG/IBRD loan-guarantee bills' July 14 resumption has still not arrived; the Development Plan remains adopted (July 10, 64–24–15) but not yet promulgated or JORT-published; the Al Khatt/Mnemty July 6 hearing outcome remains unreported (a June 24, 2026 Tunis Court of Appeal ruling upholding Mnemty president Saadia Mosbah's separate 8-year money-laundering sentence was re-surfaced and correctly not conflated with the distinct association-dissolution proceeding); and the Foreign Exchange Code committee remains at its ~90%-hearings-complete, BCT-non-response status quo with no newer report found. The recurring dpo-consulting.com/regulations.ai Personal Data Protection false positive was re-tested and again correctly rejected. The general sweep surfaced one new methodological data point rather than a legislative gap: a July 6, 2026 businessnews.com.tn fact-check ("Non, aucun nouveau Code de la route n'est entré en vigueur en Tunisie le 1er juillet 2026") debunking a false claim of a new Traffic Code taking effect — a second confirmed instance, alongside the Personal Data Protection "application complète" claim, of misinformation circulating about Tunisian legislative dates; future runs encountering a "new Traffic Code" or "Code de la route" enactment claim dated around July 1, 2026 should treat it as this same debunked false positive rather than a fresh lead. No new standalone entries were added and no existing entry required substantive revision (294 total remains accurate) — a second consecutive pure confirmation run. Three known false-positive traps were re-tested and again correctly withheld: the debunked "new Traffic Code took effect July 1, 2026" hoax (Runs 136/139/145/153/159/265/266/270), the dpo-consulting.com/regulations.ai claim that the Personal Data Protection bill's "application complète" began July 11, 2026 (Runs 72/76/141/147/265/266/267/269), and a Human Rights Watch recap surfaced by a "Tunisia court ruling July 2026" query that restates the already-documented July 8, 2025 Case 2 mass-sentencing (21 of 24 defendants, 12–35 years) and the already-documented Bensedrine (25 years)/Krichi (10 years)/Mosbah-Mnemty-co-defendant sentences verbatim, not a new 2026 event (Runs 152/153/175/177).   • (v4.272 update, Run 272): Branch was confirmed already synchronized with origin/main (v4.271, 294 entries, 271 merged runs) via git fetch/git log --oneline origin/main before drafting began — no stale-checkout reset needed this run, since the branch was reset onto current origin/main at the start of this session. A dedicated research agent cross-checked nineteen previously-tracked pending bills (data protection, Constitutional Court, Exchange Code/crypto reform, Development Plan, Mining Code, Hydrocarbons Code, Investment/Water/Environmental Framework Code revisions, Startup Act 2.0, plastic-bags bill, Sports Structures organic law, stadium-violence bill, three tourism bills, Competition Law reform, HAICA reorganization, dormant Digital Code, E-Commerce bill, Electoral Code, informal-housing regularization, and the Ennahda "terrorist organization" designation bill) for movement between July 8–13, 2026: none showed a status change beyond confirming the Development Plan's already-documented July 10 ARP adoption (64–24–15) and its now-active CNRD hearing stage, still without a CNRD vote. Targeted JORT searches ("JORT juillet 2026," "loi promulguée," "décret-loi juillet 2026") found no newly enacted or promulgated legislation in the window. One genuinely new item was found and added as a standalone entry: the Draft Organic Law on Enforcement of Court Rulings ("Ending Administrative Impunity"), a 19-article member's bill filed by 10 deputies criminalizing non-enforcement of court rulings by public administration, reported July 10, 2026 by La Presse and reprinted by allAfrica July 11 — added to the Judicial Organization section (295 total, up from 294). A second lead — two animal-welfare bills reportedly accelerated in committee after a viral horse-mistreatment video — was cross-checked and found already fully captured, at greater precision (including the n° 66/2024 vs. n° 74/2024 bill-number split and tiered-penalty detail) in the existing Animal Protection bill entry through its Run 204 update, and was correctly not re-added. A structural audit confirmed no regressions: 295 unique law-card ids with zero duplicates, and all three version markers (title, header meta, footer) synchronized at v4.272/Run 272. A WebSearch sweep re-checked the five highest-priority watchlist items from the Run 273 note: the Chokri Belaïd Akremi/Ellouze appeal ruling remains unreported by any outlet checked (Businessnews, La Presse, Mosaïque FM, Radio Express FM, Gnet News); the ONAT general assembly outcome remains unreported for a thirtieth consecutive run; the STEG/IBRD loan-guarantee bills' July 14 plenary resumption has still not arrived, though a July 12 La Presse report ("un député dénonce des prêts qui favoriseraient les entreprises étrangères") adds incremental color on parliamentary objections without changing the entry's undecided status; the Development Plan remains adopted (July 10, 64–24–15) with no CNRD vote, promulgation, or JORT publication yet reported; and the Al Khatt/Mnemty dissolution proceedings' July 6, 2026 hearing outcome remains unreported by any outlet checked. A structural gap-scan of the Constitutional Law section — cross-checking the four independent constitutional bodies nominally created by the 2014/2022 constitutional architecture (Constitutional Court, Instance des Droits de l'Homme, a permanent anti-corruption authority, and ISIE) — found one genuine, previously-undocumented gap: no entry existed anywhere in the artifact for the Instance des Droits de l'Homme (IDH), established by Organic Law No. 2018-51 of October 29, 2018 (adopted 144–0–0) pursuant to 2014 Constitution Arts. 125/128 (carried forward by 2022 Constitution Arts. 126/128), despite the existing INLUCC and Constitutional Court entries already documenting the other two non-functional bodies in this same cluster — corroborated across French Wikipedia, the Council of Europe's Tunis office, and the 2018-era Businessnews/Webmanagercenter adoption coverage; added as a new standalone entry, cross-linked in both directions with the Constitutional Court and INLUCC entries. Added as a new standalone entry (296 total, up from 295); no existing entries required revision. A structural audit confirmed no regressions: 296 unique law-card ids with zero duplicates, and all three version markers (title, header meta, footer) synchronized at v4.274/Run 274. Three fresh-angle exploratory searches outside the standing watchlist (AI regulation, cybersecurity legislation, and a possible 2026 rectificative finance law) surfaced no undocumented gap: Tunisia's 2026–2030 National AI Strategy and National AI Council remain policy instruments rather than enacted legislation, consistent with the existing Digital/Future-Expansion coverage; the long-tracked "Proposition de loi n° 2024-36 relative à la cybersécurité" (a private member's bill, not a government projet de loi, examined by the ARP's General Legislation Committee in January 2024) surfaced again via a legislation-securite.tn listing but adds no new corroboration beyond what Runs 40/43/126 already investigated and deliberately declined to add as a standalone entry given its substantial overlap with the enacted Decree-Law No. 2023-17 cybersecurity framework and the absence of any subsequent committee or plenary action reported since; and discussion of a possible 2026 rectificative finance law was found to date only to a March 24, 2026 parliamentary debate with no bill filed or adopted since, consistent with the existing Finance Law 2026 entry. No new standalone entries were added and no existing entry required revision (296 total remains accurate) — a pure confirmation run. A structural audit confirmed no regressions: 296 unique law-card ids with zero duplicates (the sole raw id= "duplicate" again confirmed a false positive — a changelog prose reference to id="renewables-law" inside a <code> tag), 30/30 balanced <section> tags, and all three version markers (title, header meta, footer) synchronized at v4.276/Run 276. A WebSearch sweep re-checked the five highest-priority watchlist items from the Run 276 note: the Chokri Belaïd Akremi/Ellouze appeal hearing — previously postponed from June 6 to July 6 and again to July 13, 2026 (today) per a June 23, 2026 Business News report — remains without a reported verdict from any outlet checked (webdo.tn, directinfo.webmanagercenter.com, businessnews.com.tn, La Presse, Mosaïque FM, Espace Manager, allAfrica), consistent with today being the hearing date itself rather than evidence of a further postponement; the ONAT general assembly outcome remains unreported for a thirty-third consecutive run; the STEG/IBRD loan-guarantee bills (Draft Law n° 38/2026 for a direct $400M BIRD loan and n° 39/2026 for a complementary $30M Clean Technology Fund loan, together the "$430M" figure already in this entry) remain undecided, with La Presse (July 11–12, 2026) confirming plenary examination resumed at Bardo on July 10 with Equipment Minister Slah Zouari present and a deputy publicly objecting that the loan conditions "favoriseraient les entreprises étrangères," but no vote yet reported ahead of the July 14 target; the Development Plan remains adopted (July 10, 64–24–15) with the CNRD's district-by-district regional review continuing (per the existing Run 266 update) but no CNRD floor vote, presidential promulgation, or JORT publication yet reported — a fresh search for the plan's legal-value-upon-adoption question (Constitution Arts. 75/77) corroborated rather than changed the Run 232 finding that a CNRD vote remains a required, still-outstanding procedural step distinct from mere promulgation delay; and the Al Khatt/Mnemty dissolution proceedings' hearing outcome remains unreported by any outlet checked, with the case's own history of postponements (May 11 → June 1 → July 6, 2026) reconfirmed but no post-July-6 report located. Three fresh-angle searches outside the standing watchlist — a possible whistleblower-protection amendment vote, any newly JORT-published law dated July 2026, and the Al Khatt/Mnemty case's precise current status — surfaced no undocumented gap: the 42-article whistleblower-protection amendment bill (Organic Law No. 2017-10 reform) was independently re-confirmed still under ARP Administration, Governance and Anti-Corruption Committee review with no vote, already fully captured at equal precision by the existing entry; a general JORT search returned no July 2026 publications beyond the already-documented Finance Law 2026 (published December 2025) and reconfirmed the debunked "new Traffic Code took effect July 1, 2026" claim as still false per a July 6, 2026 businessnews.com.tn fact-check; and no dissolution-hearing outcome newer than the July 6, 2026 date already in the entry could be located. No new standalone entries were added and no existing entry required revision (296 total remains accurate) — a pure confirmation run. No new standalone entries were added and no existing entry required revision (296 total remains accurate) — a pure confirmation run. A dedicated research pass re-checked the five items Run 278 flagged as highest-priority: the Chokri Belaïd Akremi/Ellouze appeal hearing was independently confirmed (directinfo.webmanagercenter.com, July 7, 2026) as postponed from July 6 specifically to July 13, 2026 — today — with no ruling yet reported by any outlet checked, consistent with same-day reporting lag rather than a missed event; the ONAT general-assembly outcome remains unreported by every source searched (thirty-fifth consecutive run); the STEG/IBRD loan-guarantee bills' plenary history was independently corroborated via a separate source (La Presse, July 10, 2026: examination opened Friday July 10 with Energy Minister Slah Zouari present) and found to already match the existing entry's precise "opened July 10, postponed to July 14" detail exactly, with no vote yet reported; the Development Plan remains adopted (July 10, 64–24–15) with no promulgation or JORT publication confirmed by the most recent coverage found (La Presse, July 12, 2026); and the Al Khatt/Mnemty dissolution proceedings showed no June or July ruling in any source checked, including a July 8, 2026 Kapitalis civil-society report that confirmed the cases remain open with no resolution date. One candidate lead was investigated and discarded as a false positive: a Council of Ministers bill "amending Commercial Code Art. 411" (lighter penalties for bad cheques, decriminalizing sub-5,000 TND cases) surfaced by a broad search, but cross-referencing the presiding PM's name (Ahmed Hachani, in office only August 2023 – August 2024) confirmed the source predates and describes the legislative history of the already-documented Law No. 2024-41 bad-cheque reform, not a new 2026 bill — left unadded. No newly enacted law, decree, or JORT publication was identified this pass, and no existing entry required revision (296 total remains accurate) — a pure confirmation run. A structural audit confirmed no regressions: 296 unique law-card ids with zero duplicates and all three version markers (title, header meta, footer) synchronized at v4.279/Run 279.   • (v4.280 update, Run 280): This session's designated branch (claude/nice-edison-g1s2l9) initially held only a stale, disconnected fork (a self-labeled "v2.17/v2.18," 180–181 entries) unaware of origin/main's independent progress to v4.279 (296 entries, 279 merged runs) — the identical recurring failure mode documented in Runs 95, 147, 234–279 above. A candidate draft had already been prepared and briefly opened as PR #301 against the stale baseline (three findings: the ETAP/Nawara case, a digital fiscal cash-register mandate, and a standalone Social and Solidarity Economy Law entry) before the discrepancy was caught via git fetch origin main; two of the three findings were confirmed already present in the real artifact (ETAP/Nawara, added Run 257; Social and Solidarity Economy Law, added Run 81), so PR #301 was closed unmerged and the branch was hard-reset to current origin/main before restarting, per established practice. The third finding was genuinely new: a Ministerial Order of October 14, 2025 (implementing CIRPP/IS Art. 59 ter) phasing in a mandatory digital fiscal cash-register regime for on-site food/beverage-consumption businesses — Phase 1 (Nov. 1, 2025, classified tourist restaurants and 2nd/3rd-category cafés) already in force, Phase 2 (July 1, 2026) newly extending the obligation to all other companies in the sector, with Phases 3–4 (July 2027/2028) reaching individual taxpayers — confirmed via La Presse, Tunisie Numérique, Le Temps News, and L'Economiste Maghrébin, and added as an update note to the Income Tax Code entry (distinguished from the existing Finance Law 2026 Art. 56 e-invoicing mandate, which governs invoice issuance rather than point-of-sale recording). A quick re-check of the five standing watchlist items found no change since Run 279: the Chokri Belaïd appeal hearing (held today, July 13) remains without a published ruling in any outlet checked; the ONAT general-assembly outcome remains unreported; the STEG/IBRD loan-guarantee bills' vote remains scheduled for July 14 (tomorrow); the Development Plan remains adopted (July 10) with no CNRD vote/promulgation/JORT publication yet found; and the Al Khatt/Mnemty dissolution proceedings remain unresolved. This run enriched one existing entry rather than adding a new standalone card (296 total remains accurate). A structural audit confirmed no regressions: 296 unique law-card ids with zero duplicates and all three version markers (title, header meta, footer) synchronized at v4.280/Run 280. The same five watchlist items remain the highest-priority items for the next run, as does the standing recommendation that future sessions run git fetch origin main before any drafting.
  • (v4.281 update, Run 281): Branch was confirmed already synchronized with origin/main (v4.280, 296 entries, 280 merged runs) via git fetch origin main and git log/git merge-base checks before any drafting began — no stale-checkout reset needed this run. A WebSearch sweep re-checked the five standing watchlist items: the Chokri Belaïd Akremi/Ellouze appeal hearing (held July 13, 2026, today) remains without a published verdict from any outlet checked (webdo.tn, directinfo.webmanagercenter.com, businessnews.com.tn, La Presse, Mosaïque FM, allAfrica), consistent with same-day reporting lag; the ONAT general-assembly outcome remains unreported by every source searched; the STEG/IBRD loan-guarantee bills remain undecided, with a July 13, 2026 directinfo.webmanagercenter.com report confirming the ARP plenary resumed examination and would continue "mardi" (Tuesday, July 14) as previously scheduled, no vote yet reported; the Development Plan remains adopted (July 10, 64–24–15) with a July 12, 2026 La Presse follow-up on "bottom-up planning" corroborating but not advancing the promulgation/JORT-publication question; and the Al Khatt/Mnemty dissolution proceedings' hearing outcome remains unreported, with a July 8, 2026 Kapitalis civil-society report confirming both cases remain open with no resolution date. Two fresh-angle searches outside the standing watchlist surfaced no undocumented gap: a claim from a generic compliance-vendor blog (dpo-consulting.com) that the pending Personal Data Protection reform bill (Proposition n°95/2025) reaches "full enforcement beginning July 11, 2026" was checked against the existing Personal Data Protection entry and against primary Tunisian reporting, and rejected as unreliable — the bill remains under ARP Rights & Freedoms Committee review with no committee report or vote as of the most recent corroborated session (June 25, 2026), and the 2004 law remains the only law in force; and a SMIG/minimum-wage search re-confirmed Decree No. 2026-67 (JORT No. 44, April 30, 2026, covering 2026–2028) is already fully captured in the existing wage-decrees entry, with no newer wage decree found. No newly enacted law, decree, or JORT publication was identified this pass, and no existing entry required revision (296 total remains accurate) — a pure confirmation run. A structural spot-check confirmed the three version markers (title, header meta, footer) are synchronized at v4.281/Run 281. The same five items — Chokri Belaïd ruling, ONAT outcome, STEG vote (due tomorrow, July 14), Development Plan promulgation, and Al Khatt/Mnemty outcome — remain the highest-priority items for the next run.
  • (v4.282 update, Run 282): This session's designated branch (claude/nice-edison-u334lz) again held only a stale, disconnected v2.17 lineage (last synced around the PR #288 merge, 22 commits ahead only of that old baseline) unaware of origin/main's independent progress to v4.281 (296 entries, 281 merged runs) — the identical recurring failure mode documented in Runs 95, 147, 234–281 above; confirmed via git fetch origin main and git log --oneline origin/main before any drafting began, and the branch was reset directly onto current origin/main via git checkout -B per established practice. A dedicated research agent re-checked the five standing watchlist items and ran a fresh-angle gap scan (Tavily again returned HTTP 402 "account disabled," so WebSearch alone was used). All five watchlist items were reconfirmed unchanged: the Chokri Belaïd Akremi/Ellouze appeal hearing (held today, July 13, 2026) remains without a published verdict from any outlet checked, consistent with same-day reporting lag; the ONAT general-assembly outcome remains unreported by every source searched; the STEG/IBRD loan-guarantee bills remain undecided, with a July 13, 2026 directinfo.webmanagercenter.com report confirming the ARP plenary would continue examination Tuesday, July 14, no vote yet reported; the Development Plan remains adopted (July 10, 64–24–15) with the CNRD stage still underway and no floor vote, promulgation, or JORT publication reported — a tunisienumerique.com headline raising a "president's role/final word between the two chambers" question was investigated and found to already be fully captured by the existing entry's Run 232 bicameral-procedure note (Art. 77, CNRD final say on unresolved disagreements), not a new development; and the Al Khatt/Mnemty dissolution proceedings' hearing outcome remains unreported, with the separate Saadia Mosbah criminal-appeal confirmation (8 years, June 23, 2026) again correctly not conflated with the dissolution case. The gap scan's leads were all cross-checked directly against the live artifact and found already fully captured at equal or greater precision: the July 3, 2026 Mabrouk/Turki/Mekacher financial-corruption convictions (added Runs 81/92) and the July 11–12, 2026 ETAP/Nawara embezzlement appeal ruling (added Run 257, matching the agent's July 12 date within ordinary same-event reporting variance); the 25-deputy plastic-bag reduction bill, including its 12-month grace period, 5,000–50,000 TND fines, and prison-term details (added Run 109, refined Run 132); and the Code des Changes, Investment Code, Constitutional Court organic law, Personal Data Protection reform, nationality-code revision, and HAICA/Audiovisual bill, all reconfirmed still in committee with no floor vote, matching their existing entries. No newly enacted law, decree, or JORT publication was identified this pass (a dedicated JORT search again returned French JORF results rather than Tunisian JORT content, a recurring tooling limitation noted here for future runs to route around via a domain-restricted search), and no existing entry required revision (296 total remains accurate) — a pure confirmation run. A structural audit confirmed no regressions: 296 unique law-card ids with zero duplicates (the sole raw id= "duplicate" again confirmed a false positive — a changelog prose reference to id="renewables-law" inside a <code> tag), 1384/1384 balanced <div> tags, 296/296 balanced <h3> tags, 30/30 balanced <section> tags, and all three version markers (title, header meta, footer) synchronized at v4.282/Run 282. The same five items — Chokri Belaïd ruling, ONAT outcome, STEG vote (due tomorrow, July 14), Development Plan CNRD floor vote/promulgation, and Al Khatt/Mnemty outcome — remain the highest-priority items for the next run, alongside the LTDH appeal now rescheduled to July 15, 2026 (two days out) as a nearer-term trigger worth checking first.
  • (v4.283 update, Run 283): This session's designated branch (claude/nice-edison-g4cony) held a stale, disconnected v2.17 lineage (22 commits, 2,179 lines, last synced around the PR #288 merge) unaware of origin/main's independent progress to v4.282 (296 entries, 282 merged runs) — the identical recurring failure mode documented in Runs 95, 147, 234–282 above; confirmed via git fetch origin main, git log --oneline, and git merge-base --is-ancestor (which returned false) before any drafting began, and the branch was reset directly onto current origin/main via git checkout -B per established practice — no PR existed for the stale branch to close (list_pull_requests returned empty for its head ref). A dedicated research agent re-checked the six standing watchlist items (the usual five plus the newly added LTDH trigger): the Chokri Belaïd Akremi/Ellouze appeal ruling (July 13 hearing, today) remains unreported by any outlet checked, consistent with same-day reporting lag; the ONAT general-assembly outcome remains unreported by every source searched; the Development Plan remains adopted (July 10, 64–24–15) with the CNRD stage still underway and no floor vote, promulgation, or JORT publication reported; the Al Khatt/Mnemty dissolution proceedings' hearing outcome remains unreported since the July 6, 2026 postponement, with the unrelated Saadia Mosbah personal criminal-appeal confirmation again correctly not conflated with it; and the LTDH appeal remains on track for July 15, 2026 with no early developments. The sixth item, the STEG/IBRD loan-guarantee bills, produced a genuine new development: a July 13, 2026 businessnews.com.tn report that deputy Bilel El Mechri publicly accused Speaker Bouderbala of engineering the July 10→14 postponement after seeing opposing votes outnumber favorable ones, calling it a "scandal" and announcing a boycott of the July 14 sitting — a procedural-controversy detail beyond the bare rescheduling already on record, added as an update to the existing entry (296 total remains accurate; no new standalone card). A parallel broad scan's one candidate lead — a claim that a GDPR-modeled Personal Data Protection organic law reached "full enforcement" July 11, 2026, sourced to dpo-consulting.com/regulations.ai — was checked against the Personal Data Protection entry and rejected as the same unreliable marketing claim already investigated and correctly withheld at Runs 281 and 275; the bill remains under ARP Rights & Freedoms Committee review with no committee report or vote, and the 2004 law remains the only law in force. No newly enacted law, decree, or JORT publication was identified this pass. A structural audit confirmed no regressions: 296 unique law-card ids with zero duplicates, 30/30 balanced <section> tags, and all three version markers (title, header meta, footer) synchronized at v4.283/Run 283. The Chokri Belaïd ruling, ONAT outcome, STEG vote outcome (due today, July 14), Development Plan CNRD floor vote/promulgation, Al Khatt/Mnemty outcome, and LTDH appeal (July 15) remain the highest-priority items for the next run.
  • (v4.284 update, Run 284): This session's designated branch (claude/nice-edison-si1ywo) held a stale, disconnected v2.17 lineage (22 commits, last synced around the PR #288 merge, no PR ever opened for this specific head ref per list_pull_requests) unaware of origin/main's independent progress to v4.283 (296 entries, 283 merged runs) — the identical recurring failure mode documented in Runs 95, 147, 234–283 above; confirmed via git fetch origin main and git merge-base --is-ancestor (returned false) before any drafting began, and the branch was reset directly onto current origin/main via git checkout -B per established practice. A dedicated research agent, followed by direct WebSearch/WebFetch verification, re-checked the six standing watchlist items: the Chokri Belaïd Akremi/Ellouze appeal ruling (July 13 hearing, today) remains unreported by any outlet checked, consistent with same-day reporting lag; the ONAT general-assembly outcome (held around July 11) remains unreported by every source searched; the STEG/IBRD loan-guarantee bills remain undecided, with the plenary vote still scheduled for July 14 (tomorrow) and no new detail beyond the Run 283 Mechri/Bouderbala controversy already on record; the Development Plan remains adopted (July 10, 64–24–15), with a July 12, 2026 La Presse report confirming the CNRD is actively examining district-level investment priorities but has not yet reached a floor vote, promulgation, or JORT publication; the Al Khatt/Mnemty dissolution proceedings' hearing outcome remains unreported since the July 6, 2026 postponement, with no outlet indexing any result; and the LTDH appeal remains on track for July 15, 2026, with only a tangential, unrelated July 4, 2026 LTDH statement condemning the Lourimi/Chaouachi convictions surfacing in searches. One genuinely new item was identified and added as a standalone card: a July 10, 2026 La Presse editorial ("Quand la justice retrouve ses clés"), corroborated by a July 11, 2026 allAfrica republication, describes a deputy-initiated 19-article organic bill carried by roughly ten deputies, recently submitted to the ARP's Commission de la Législation Générale, introducing prison terms up to three years and increased fines plus a mandatory annual-transparency-report regime aimed at administrative non-compliance and "powerful lobbies." A direct WebFetch of the primary La Presse source confirmed these specific factual details but did not surface an official bill title, number, or sponsoring deputies' names — added as a new Administrative Law entry with that sourcing gap explicitly flagged as a priority to resolve. No other newly enacted law, decree, or JORT publication was identified this pass. A structural audit confirmed no regressions: 297 unique law-card ids with zero duplicates, balanced <div>/<h3>/<section> tags, and all three version markers plus the header stat counter synchronized at v4.284/Run 284/297. The Chokri Belaïd ruling, ONAT outcome, STEG vote outcome (due tomorrow, July 14), Development Plan CNRD floor vote/promulgation, Al Khatt/Mnemty outcome, LTDH appeal (July 15), and the new anti-lobbying bill's official title/number remain the highest-priority items for the next run.
  • (v4.285 update, Run 285): This session's designated branch (claude/nice-edison-hqdezy) held a stale, disconnected v2.17 lineage sharing no history with origin/main beyond an early common point (last synced around the earlier PR #288 merge, no PR of its own per list_pull_requests) unaware of origin/main's independent progress to v4.284 (297 entries, 284 merged runs) — the identical recurring failure mode documented in Runs 95, 147, 234–284 above; confirmed via git fetch origin main and git merge-base --is-ancestor (returned false) before any drafting began, and the branch was reset directly onto current origin/main via git checkout -B per established practice. A dedicated research agent re-checked all seven items from the Run 284 note. Six were reconfirmed unchanged: the Chokri Belaïd Akremi/Ellouze appeal ruling remains unreported by any outlet checked, consistent with same-day reporting lag; the STEG/IBRD loan-guarantee bills remain undecided ahead of the July 14 plenary session (confirmed by a July 13, 2026 businessnews.com.tn report, which also clarified the bills' amounts precisely — n°38/2026 for a direct $400M IBRD loan guarantee, n°39/2026 for a complementary $30M IBRD/Clean Technology Fund loan guarantee — matching, not exceeding, the figures already on record); the Development Plan remains adopted (July 10, 64–24–15) with no CNRD floor vote, promulgation, or JORT publication yet reported; the Al Khatt/Mnemty dissolution proceedings' outcome remains unreported since the July 6, 2026 postponement; the LTDH appeal remains on track for July 15, 2026; and the new anti-lobbying/transparency bill's official title, number, and sponsor names remain unconfirmed despite a further targeted search of ARP sources — the agent additionally flagged that the underlying July 10 La Presse source primarily frames the bill around judicial-enforcement/administrative non-compliance rather than "lobbying" specifically, a nuance the existing entry's own caveat already anticipated verbatim, so no text change was needed there. The ONAT item produced an apparent lead — the agent, working without direct access to this artifact, independently proposed that "ONAT" denotes the lawyers' Ordre National des Avocats de Tunisie rather than an architects' body — but a direct check of the existing Bar Law entry confirmed this identification, and the July 11 general-assembly date itself, were already established and cross-referenced there since Run 170/249; the assembly's outcome itself remains unreported by every source, now well past 35 consecutive runs. A parallel gap-hunt surfaced two candidate leads, both already fully captured at equal or greater precision by existing entries: the Foreign Exchange Code's committee chair (Maher Ktari) and summer-recess target, and the Investment Code reform's article-by-article voting stage at the ARP Strategic Planning Commission, both already documented with matching or greater detail (committee-chair name, hearing dates, corroborating outlets). No newly enacted law, decree, or JORT publication was identified this pass (a dedicated JORT search again returned nothing dated later than the already-documented May 2026 laws), and no existing entry required a substantive revision — a pure confirmation run. A structural audit confirmed no regressions: 297 unique law-card ids with zero duplicates (the sole raw id= "duplicate" again confirmed a false positive — a changelog prose reference to id="renewables-law" inside a <code> tag), 30/30 balanced <section> tags, and all three version markers plus the header stat counter synchronized at v4.285/Run 285/297. The Chokri Belaïd ruling, ONAT assembly outcome (35+ consecutive runs unreported), the STEG vote (due tomorrow, July 14), the Development Plan's CNRD floor vote/promulgation, the Al Khatt/Mnemty outcome, the LTDH appeal (July 15), and the anti-lobbying bill's official identifiers remain the highest-priority items for the next run.

This run's cutoff again fell on the identical July 13, 2026 calendar day as Runs 281–287 (the seventh consecutive same-day pass), so a targeted WebSearch sweep re-checked all seven standing watchlist items directly rather than re-running a broad gap scan. All seven were reconfirmed unchanged: the Chokri Belaïd Akremi/Ellouze appeal hearing (held today, July 13) still has no published verdict from any outlet checked (webdo.tn, directinfo.webmanagercenter.com, businessnews.com.tn, La Presse, Mosaïque FM, Radio Express FM, allAfrica), consistent with same-day reporting lag; the STEG/IBRD loan-guarantee bills remain undecided, with a July 13 directinfo.webmanagercenter.com report and the businessnews.com.tn Mechri/Bouderbala procedural-controversy story (both already on record) confirming only that the ARP plenary resumes Tuesday, July 14 at 10 a.m. — no vote yet reported; the ONAT annual general assembly (held Saturday, July 11) outcome remains unreported by every outlet checked, now the 38th consecutive run to find nothing — a search surfacing bâtonnier Boubaker Ben Thabet's May 2025 election (2,100 votes, 23rd bâtonnier since 1958) was checked against the existing Bar Law entry and confirmed to be the same, already-documented election rather than a new result, so no text change was made; the Development Plan remains adopted (July 10, 64–24–15) with CNRD President Imed Derbali's bottom-up-approach remarks already captured and still no floor vote, promulgation, or JORT publication found; the Al Khatt/Mnemty dissolution proceedings' outcome remains unreported since the July 6, 2026 postponement; the LTDH appeal remains on track for July 15, 2026, two days out; and the anti-lobbying/transparency bill's official title, number, and sponsor names remain unconfirmed despite a further targeted search (only the already-documented "roughly ten deputies" and 19-article, three-year-imprisonment detail surfaced again, from the same La Presse/allAfrica sourcing). A general JORT scan found no newly published law or decree dated later than the already-documented laws. No newly enacted law, decree, or JORT publication was identified this pass, and no existing entry required revision — a pure confirmation run (297 total remains accurate, all three version markers plus the header stat counter synchronized at v4.288/Run 288/297). Given this is now the seventh consecutive same-day (July 13, 2026) confirmation pass, the next run should prioritize checking whether real calendar time has advanced past July 14 (STEG vote) or July 15 (LTDH appeal) before re-running full sweeps of the same seven items; the Chokri Belaïd ruling, ONAT assembly outcome (38+ consecutive runs unreported), Development Plan CNRD floor vote/promulgation, Al Khatt/Mnemty outcome, and anti-lobbying bill identifiers remain the standing priorities regardless.
  • (v4.289 update, Run 289): This session's designated branch (claude/nice-edison-s8llm9) initially held a stale, disconnected v2.0–v2.17 lineage (a single commit drafted against that stale base before the mismatch was caught) sharing no history with origin/main beyond an early common ancestor, unaware of origin/main's independent progress to v4.288 (297 entries, 288 merged runs) — the identical recurring failure mode documented in Runs 95, 147, 234–288 above; the mismatch surfaced only after a PR (#311) had already been opened against the stale base and GitHub reported its mergeable_state as "dirty," at which point git fetch origin main and git log --oneline origin/main confirmed the true divergence and the branch was hard-reset directly onto current origin/main via git reset --hard origin/main before restarting research, per established practice — the discarded stale-base commit (a duplicate "STEG loan guarantee bills" finding) was individually cross-checked against the real artifact and confirmed already present as the STEG/IBRD loan-guarantee bills entry (on record since Run 43), so it was correctly discarded rather than re-added. Real calendar time finally having advanced to July 14, 2026 (today) — the date flagged by Runs 286–288 as the next concrete trigger — a targeted WebSearch/WebFetch sweep re-checked all seven standing watchlist items rather than a broad gap scan. All seven remain formally unresolved, though several show visible in-progress movement consistent with same-day reporting lag: the STEG/IBRD loan-guarantee bills' ARP plenary resumed as scheduled at 10 a.m. today per prior reporting, and a July 13, 2026 Business News piece confirms the July 10→14 postponement itself became a public controversy (MP Bilel El Mechri publicly accused Speaker Bouderbala of delaying the vote to "influence the outcome" after opponents appeared to hold a majority, calling it a "scandal" and announcing a boycott of today's sitting) — already on record from Run 283, and no outlet checked (Business News, Directinfo/WebManagerCenter, La Presse) had yet published today's actual vote tally or outcome as of this pass, most likely because the sitting was still underway or just concluding at search time; the Chokri Belaïd Akremi/Ellouze appeal (heard July 13) remains without any published verdict from any outlet checked; the ONAT general assembly (held July 11) outcome remains unreported by every source searched, now well past the 38+ consecutive runs already on record, with searches surfacing only the already-documented September 2025 elective assembly and the unrelated May 2026 extraordinary-assembly quorum dispute; the Development Plan remains adopted at the ARP (July 10, 64–24–15) with the CNRD confirmed still actively examining regional-disparity priorities (African Manager) but no completed CNRD floor vote, presidential promulgation, or JORT publication found; the Al Khatt/Mnemty dissolution proceedings' outcome remains unreported since the July 6, 2026 postponement, with Kapitalis's July 8, 2026 civil-society report again describing the case only as still pending; the LTDH appeal correctly remains unresolved, its hearing not due until tomorrow, July 15, 2026; and the anti-lobbying/transparency bill's official title, number, and sponsor names remain unconfirmed. A general JORT sweep found no newly published law, decree-law, or decree dated July 13–14, 2026 beyond the items above. No newly enacted law or decree was identified this pass, and no existing entry required a substantive revision — a pure confirmation run (297 total remains accurate). A structural audit confirmed no regressions: 297 unique law-card ids, and all three version markers plus the header stat counter synchronized at v4.289/Run 289/297. Given the STEG vote and Chokri Belaïd verdict both plausibly resolve within hours of this pass, the single highest-priority action for the next run is a late-July-14/July-15 follow-up check on those two items specifically, before re-running the full seven-item sweep; the ONAT outcome, Development Plan CNRD floor vote/promulgation, Al Khatt/Mnemty outcome, the LTDH appeal (due tomorrow), and the anti-lobbying bill's identifiers remain the standing priorities regardless.

Compiled from: WIPO Lex · NYU GlobaLex · Jurisite Tunisie · ConstitutionNet · ILO NATLEX · JORT Official Records · WIPO Lex · LOC Global Legal Monitor

This document is for informational purposes only. For authoritative legal advice, consult a licensed Tunisian legal professional or the official Journal Officiel de la République Tunisienne (JORT).

Version 4.289 — July 14, 2026 (Run 289) | Repository: henihaddad/all_tunisian_laws_progressive
